reason_for_classification: This video depicts a VR/digital simulation exercise involving navigating a scope (left controller) to find numbered cubes and touch them with an instrument (right controller) while waiting for visual feedback (ring turning green). This is not one of the five official FLS MISTELS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture).
content_type: Educational/tutorial demonstrating a laparoscopic simulator training module

frame_analyses.description: Title card: '2.5 30 Degrees scope orientation' — indicates scope angle setup instruction

Narrative instruction: 'Navigate with your scope to find the numbers on the cubes. The number will show up once you touch the cube with your right controller. Focus on the number until the ring of the scope turns green.'
Visual demonstration of controller roles: left controller labeled 'Use your left controller as a scope', right controller labeled 'Use your right controller as an instrument'
Gameplay showing scope targeting cube with number '1' visible; instruction 'Touch and hold the cube with your right controller, a number will show up. Focus on the number until the ring turns green.'
Scope now highlighting cube with number '3' displayed; status shows 'Find the next number (2/10)' — demonstrating progression through sequenced targets
Scope navigates to different cube positions; instruction 'Rotate the camera to look around the corner' — demonstrates spatial navigation within 3D environment
Multiple cubes visible with white labels (completion markers); scope circles different targets; status 'Find the next number (5/10)' showing mid-task progress
Final targets being located; LAP simulator branding visible in lower right corner; status 'Find the next number (8/10)'
Scope targeting final cube positions; demonstrates smooth navigation and targeting mechanics throughout task sequence

scoring_rationale: This is NOT an official FLS MISTELS task. It appears to be a custom or commercial laparoscopic simulator training module focused on scope navigation and target identification in a virtual environment (possibly using the LAP Simulator platform, as evidenced by branding). It does not match any of the five standardized FLS tasks and therefore cannot be scored using the official FLS rubric.

demonstrated_techniques: Scope navigation using left-hand control (laparoscopic camera positioning)
Identifying and targeting objects under magnified/scope view
Maintaining focus on numbered targets until completion feedback received
Camera rotation and multi-angle viewing to locate hidden targets
verbal_cues: Navigate with your scope to find the numbers on the cubes
The number will show up once you touch the cube with your right controller
Focus on the number until the ring of the scope turns green
Touch and hold the cube with your right controller
Rotate the camera to look around the corner
common_errors_addressed: Failure to maintain proper scope orientation (emphasized by initial '30 degrees' setup instruction)
Inability to locate hidden/obscured targets (addressed by explicit instruction to rotate camera)
Incomplete focus on target (addressed by requirement to hold until ring turns green)

summary: This video is an instructional/tutorial for a custom laparoscopic simulator training module that teaches scope navigation and target identification. It is NOT one of the five official FLS MISTELS tasks and therefore does not receive an FLS score. The content is educational in nature with clear narrative guidance, visual overlays, and interactive demonstrations of proper laparoscopic camera and instrument control techniques.
